violations.text: Molluscan Shellfish (clams) located in the prep unit did not have the proper identification tags. Molluscan Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the Molluscan Shellstock are received until the container is empty. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets of the prep unit were observed in poor repair, splitting away from the doors. Gaskets must be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2025

The cutting board of the prep unit is very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2025

Shelving in the storage area is made of wood. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Wood is to be covered using materials that are smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and nonabsorbent. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dishwasher at the bar. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2025

Soap and paper towels were not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand soap and paper towels at all times. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2024

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2024

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2024

The cutting board of the prep unit is very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Owner/operator removed cutting board during inspection and will be using individual cutting boards. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets of the prep unit were observed in poor repair, splitting away from the doors. Gaskets are to be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024

Shelving in the kitchen and storage room is made of wood. Wood is not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Wood is to be covered using materials that are smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024
Leak observed under the 3-bay sink. Plumbing must be maintained in good repair. Service call has been placed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2024
The floor in the kitchen and storage area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2024
